title: MTT / CellTiter cell-viability plate (384-well)
description: 384-well cell-viability dose-response layout (MTT / MTS / CellTiter): no-cell blanks, untreated vehicle (100% viability), max-kill positive control (0% viability), and a 20-point treatment dose series.

payload.description: MTT / MTS / CellTiter cell-viability plate for a 384-well plate (16 rows A-P x 24 columns), following standard tetrazolium-assay practice and the NCBI Assay Guidance Manual cell-viability chapter (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK144065/). Column 1 = no-cell blanks (medium + reagent, no cells) for background subtraction. Column 2 = untreated/vehicle controls (maximum-viability 100% reference). Column 3 = max-kill positive control (minimum-viability 0% reference). Columns 4-23 = a 20-point compound dose series (highest dose column 4, descending to column 23); each row A-P is an independent treatment, compound, or replicate. Column 24 = medium border to limit edge effects. Normalize each treated well as (well - blank) / (vehicle mean - blank). Read MTT formazan at 570 nm (or per kit). Run >= 3 replicates per condition.

region_labels.notes: Medium + MTT/MTS reagent, no cells. Background absorbance subtracted from all other wells.
Cells + vehicle only. Maximum-viability reference; normalize treated wells to the mean of this column.
Cells + a cytotoxic agent (e.g. 10% DMSO, 0.1% Triton X-100, or staurosporine). Minimum-viability / fully-killed reference. Standard practice, not part of the basic kit protocol.

Test-compound 20-point dose series, highest dose in column 4 descending to column 23. Each row A-P is an independent treatment (compound or cell line) or a replicate; aim for >= 3 replicates per condition.
Medium-only column to limit evaporation and edge effects, or use for a second compound. Outer wells are prone to edge effects (NCBI Assay Guidance Manual).
